heading: Demonstration, support, and research projects for multigenerational and civic engagement activities

children.label: A
children.text: meaningful roles for participants;

children.label: B
children.text: reciprocity in relationship building;

children.label: C
children.text: reduced social isolation and improved participant social connectedness;

children.label: D
children.text: improved economic well-being for older individuals;

children.label: E
children.text: increased lifelong learning; or

children.label: i
children.text: providing support for older relative caregivers (as defined in ) raising children (such as support for kinship navigator programs); orsection 3030s(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: involving volunteers who are older individuals who provide support and information to families who have a child with a disability or chronic illness, or other families in need of such family support;
coordinate multigenerational activities and civic engagement activities, including multigenerational nutrition and meal service programs;
promote volunteerism, including by providing opportunities for older individuals to become a mentor to individuals in younger generations; and
facilitate development of, and participation in, multigenerational activities and civic engagement activities.

children.heading: Grant and contract periods
children.label: b Grant and contract periods
children.text: Each grant awarded and contract entered into under subsection (a) shall be for a period of not less than 36 months.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible organization shall use funds made available under a grant awarded, or a contract entered into, under this section to carry out a project described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Provision of projects through grantees
children.label: 2 Provision of projects through grantees
children.text: In awarding grants and entering into contracts under this section, the Assistant Secretary shall ensure that such grants and contracts are for the projects that satisfy each requirement under paragraphs (1) through (4) of subsection (a).

eligible organizations with a demonstrated record of carrying out, intent to carry out, or intent to partner with local organizations or multiservice organizations to carry out, multigenerational activities or civic engagement activities;

eligible organizations proposing multigenerational activity projects that will serve older individuals and communities with the greatest need (with particular attention to low-income minority individuals, older individuals with limited English proficiency, older individuals residing in rural areas, and low-income minority communities);
eligible organizations proposing civic engagement projects that will serve communities with the greatest need;
eligible organizations with the capacity to develop meaningful roles and assignments that use the time, skills, and experience of older individuals to serve public and nonprofit organizations; and
eligible organizations proposing multigenerational activity projects that utilize shared site programs, such as collocated child care and long-term care facilities.

children.heading: Application
children.label: e Application
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant or enter into a contract under subsection (a), an organization shall submit an application to the Assistant Secretary at such time, in such manner, and accompanied by such information as the Assistant Secretary may reasonably require.
be a State, an area agency on aging, or an organization that provides opportunities for older individuals to participate in activities described in such subsection; and
have the capacity to conduct the coordination, promotion, and facilitation described in such subsection through the use of multigenerational coordinators.

children.label: A
children.text: the effectiveness of such activities;

children.label: B
children.text: the impact of such activities on the community being served and the organization providing the activities; and

children.label: C
children.text: the impact of such activities on older individuals participating in such projects.

children.label: A
children.text: the names or descriptive titles of the projects funded under subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the nature and operation of such projects;

children.label: C
children.text: the names and addresses of organizations that conducted such projects;

children.label: D
children.text: a description of the methods and success of such projects in recruiting older individuals as employees and as volunteers to participate in the projects;

children.label: E
children.text: a description of the success of the projects in retaining older individuals participating in such projects as employees and as volunteers;

children.label: F
children.text: the rate of turnover of older individuals who are employees or volunteers in such projects;

children.label: G
children.text: a strategy for disseminating the findings resulting from such projects; and

children.label: H
children.text: any policy change recommendations relating to such projects.

children.heading: Multigenerational activity
children.label: 1 Multigenerational activity
children.text: The term “multigenerational activity” means an activity that provides an opportunity for interaction between 2 or more individuals of different generations, including activities connecting older individuals and youth in a child care program, a youth day care program, an educational assistance program, an at-risk youth intervention program, a juvenile delinquency treatment program, a before- or after-school program, a library program, or a family support program.

children.label: A
children.text: builds the capacity of public and nonprofit organizations to develop meaningful roles and assignments, that use the time, skill, and experience of older individuals to serve those organizations; and

children.label: i
children.text: older individuals; and

children.label: ii
children.text: individuals in younger generations.
